meta.title: Novae · Let every new star be seen
meta.description: Novae helps every student proposal be seen—and turn into real campus change.

hero.eyebrow: Every voice can become the start of change
hero.title: Let every new star on campus be seen.
hero.lede: Forms vanish. Posts scroll away. Novae keeps proposals, support, review, and responses on one traceable path—so students know where things stand and schools know what is next.

story.title:
One new star begins.
A constellation creates change.
story.body: Novae is the plural of nova: new stars that suddenly brighten the night sky. Every student proposal can be one of those stars. Seen and supported together, they form a constellation capable of changing a school.

features.title:
More than
another form.
features.lede: Privacy rules, support thresholds, review status, announcements, and notifications live in one configurable system—so students and staff share the same record.

cards.title: Privacy by context
cards.body: Each category decides who can read and whether authors appear. Shared links never bypass login or server-side access rules.

cards.title: Support when it matters
cards.body: Enable goals and deadlines for public campaigns; turn support off for facility reports or private cases. Cancel anytime and see live progress.

cards.title: School-domain Google login
cards.body: Sign in with a verified Google account on the allowed domain. Admin roles sync server-side—the browser is never the authority.

cards.title: A clear status lifecycle
cards.body: Under review, rejected, processing, completed, or infeasible—with response targets and written outcomes on one timeline.

cards.title: Public ideas, private cases
cards.body: Campus-wide ideas can gather support, while sensitive rights cases stay visible only to the student and responsible staff.

cards.title: Notifications re-check access
cards.body: In-app and Web Push cover comments, reviews, and announcements. Opening a notification re-checks access so private content stays closed.

cards.title: Announcements that broadcast
cards.body: Admins publish and edit; school users can read, like, and comment—with optional push delivery.

cards.title: A shared view for response teams
cards.body: Pending work, approaching deadlines, and participation appear together so nothing quietly disappears.

workflow.title: From a voice to a traceable outcome.
workflow.lede: Students know what happens next. Administrators know what needs attention. The exact path still depends on each category's configuration.

steps.title: Submit
steps.body: Choose a topic, understand who can see it, and clearly describe the change you want.

steps.title: Review
steps.body: When review is required, admins approve publication or record a concrete rejection reason.

steps.title: Participate
steps.body: Authorized users may support (when enabled), comment, and follow progress.

steps.title: Process
steps.body: Admins update status and response targets, notifying authors and related users.

steps.title: Resolve
steps.body: Close as completed or infeasible with a written outcome students can revisit.

config.title:
One platform.
Different rules per topic.
config.lede: You do not need a second system for private cases. Configure visibility, authorship, and support—then retune as your school evolves.
points: Public campaigns: school-visible with optional support goals
Reviewed then public: internal gate before campus participation
Private cases: author and admin only, support off

access.title: School-wide
access.body: Signed-in school users can browse by list and status rules.

access.title: Reviewed, then public
access.body: Author and admins until approved; then campus visibility.

access.title: Owner & admin
access.body: Sensitive rights cases stay off the public board.

snippets.public:
readAccess: school
authorVisible: true
support:
  enabled: true
  goal: 50
  deadlineDays: 14
snippets.private:
readAccess: owner-admin
authorVisible: false
support:
  enabled: false
snippets.facility:
readAccess: school
authorVisible: true
support:
  enabled: false

public.title: Campus can support
public.body: Hitting the goal starts the configured process with live progress.

private.title: Off the public list
private.body: Only author and admins can read; admins still retain the author link.

facility.title: Fast reports, no petition
facility.body: Support off—focus on status and site photos.

experience.title:
One workflow.
Clear on every screen.
experience.lede: Desktop keeps complete columns and efficient controls. Mobile reprioritizes information and provides bottom navigation with a create entry.

architecture.title:
Access is not in the browser.
It is in server rules.
architecture.lede: The UI is the experience layer. Who can read and who can manage is decided by server-side rules and category config—shared links cannot bypass them.
architecture.note: Novae is self-hosted open source, not a managed SaaS. Adopters own secrets, privacy notices, and incident response.

pwa.title:
Open like a website.
Keep it like an app.
pwa.lede: No app store required. Supported browsers offer installation at the right moment, then launch from the home screen. Push still needs a separate permission.

cards.title: A considerate install prompt
cards.body: Shown only when supported, with a clear option to decide later.

cards.title: Made for mobile and desktop
cards.body: Full tables on desktop; bottom navigation, reprioritized rows, and a center create action on mobile.

cards.title: Updates without disruption
cards.body: A clear prompt when an update is ready; the latest version takes over after reopening.

cards.title: Notifications stay in your control
cards.body: Device notifications can be enabled or disabled anytime. Important updates remain in-app without push.

roles.title:
Students speak.
Teams can respond.
roles.lede: Roles shape the experience and the permission boundary. Hiding a button is UX only—authorization always lives on the server.

cards.title: School users
items: Manage their own content and notifications

cards.title: Administrators
items: Use the admin dashboard to prioritize work

more.title: Board & search
more.body: Browse by category, status, and title. My proposals keeps what you can still read.

more.title: Discussion in context
more.body: Questions and replies stay beside the proposal—not scattered across chats.

more.title: Photos of the site
more.body: Facility and routing issues become clear at a glance.

more.title: Open to reshape
more.body: MIT licensed. Fork it and build participation with your own rules.

fit.title:
Know whether
it fits your campus.
fit.lede: Novae is a self-hosted application, not a managed service. Adopters own cloud resources, secrets, privacy notices, admin appointment, and incident response.
yes: Student governments needing public proposals and support
Mixing public issues with private rights cases
Small teams running one school on managed cloud
no: No external cloud or fully air-gapped hosting
E-signatures, statutory appeals, or formal document exchange
Multi-tenant billing or cross-school SaaS isolation
No one to own secrets, billing, and security events
fit.footnote: The repo includes build, test, and delivery workflows—not an SLA or compliance certification—and does not promise a stable public API. Prepare a response team, clear privacy notices, and ongoing maintenance before adopting.

cta.eyebrow: MIT LICENSE · SELF-HOST · MAKE IT YOURS
cta.title:
Turn campus voices
into visible progress.
cta.body: Start with the documentation, or fork the project and build a participation platform with your own category rules.

footer.tagline: Open source for transparent, traceable campus participation.

issues.title: Add charging outlets in dorm common areas

issues.author: Anonymous
issues.title: Add a safe late-night campus shuttle route
